title: Warm up
content: This introductory slide presents three illustrated scenes of taxi drivers viewing different Chinese cities through their car windows. Each image represents a distinct city with unique visual cues: Beijing shows traditional architecture alongside modern highways, Chongqing shows mountain cable cars and traditional buildings, and Hangzhou shows a lake with a driver using a smartphone with AI/tech elements.
key_claims: Beijing is depicted with traditional architecture, city traffic, and modern skyscrapers
Chongqing is depicted with mountainside buildings and cable cars
Hangzhou is depicted with a lake view and smart technology/AI elements in the car

title: An Evolving Nation
content: This title slide introduces the presentation's theme, 'A Ride Through China,' using imagery of a taxi sign and iconic Chinese landmarks. The images include the Forbidden City, a nighttime cityscape resembling Chongqing's Hongya Cave, and a pagoda representing Hangzhou's Leifeng Pagoda.
key_claims: The presentation frames China's development through the metaphor of a taxi ride
Featured landmarks include the Forbidden City, Hongya Cave-style architecture, and a traditional pagoda

title: Session 1 Match the City Chain! Investigate and research
content: This slide outlines the structure for the session's research activity, using an isometric city illustration paired with a three-step process flow. The flow guides students from identifying local features to development strategies and finally to outcomes and positive impacts.
key_claims: The research process consists of three sequential steps: Local Features, Development Strategies, and Outcomes & Positive Impact
Students will investigate and research city development using this framework

title: Match the city to its development strategy
content: This activity slide asks students to match three Chinese cities—Beijing, Chongqing, and Hangzhou—to their respective development strategies. The correct matches are shown with red lines connecting each city to its strategy, accompanied by representative images of each city's landmarks.
key_claims: Beijing is matched with 'Tourism & Economic growth'
Chongqing is matched with 'Technology & tradition integration (融合)'
Hangzhou is matched with 'Sustainable urban development'

title: Through the eyes of taxi drivers…
content: This slide summarizes the development strategies of three cities as seen from a taxi driver's perspective, reiterating the images from the warm-up slide with corrected labels. It concludes with the overarching theme that China is an evolving nation where each city tailors strategies to its local features, referencing the Chinese principle '因地制宜' (adapting to local conditions).
key_claims: Beijing's strategy focuses on sustainable urban development
Chongqing's strategy focuses on tourism and economic growth
Hangzhou's strategy focuses on technology and tradition integration
China is described as an evolving nation with unique development strategies per city
Each city develops strategies based on local features to promote city development (因地制宜)
